questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some desks are tables. All tables are icecreams. Some icecreams are drums. Conclusions: I. Some tables are icecreams. II. Some desks are icecreams. III. Some desks are drums. IV. Some desks are not drums.

choices.c: I and either III or IV follow
choices.d: I, II and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No killer is a sweater. No jacket is a sweater. Some jackets are roses. Conclusions: I. Some roses are sweaters. II. Some roses are not sweaters. III. No killer is a jacket. IV. Some jackets are killers.

choices.a: Either I or II and III follow
choices.b: Either II or IV and either I or II follow
choices.d: Either I or II and either III or IV follow
choices.e: None of the above is true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All blondes are beautiful. All brunettes are beautiful. All brunettes are cute. Conclusions: I. Some cute are beautiful. II. Some blondes are cute. III. Some blondes are brunettes. IV. Some brunettes are not cute.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some boxes are chocolates. Some tables are desks. No box is a desk. Conclusions: I. Some chocolates are desks. II. Some chocolates are not desks. III. Some tables are boxes. IV. Some tables are not boxes.

choices.b: Either I or II, and either III or IV follow
choices.d: Any three of the above follow
choices.e: None of the above is true

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some drums are baskets. All baskets are gaskets. Conclusions: I. All gaskets are drums. II. Some gaskets are drums. III. Some gaskets are baskets. IV. Some gaskets are not baskets.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No paper is a graper. All grapers are takers. Conclusions: I. Some papers are not takers. II. Some papers are takers. III. Some takers are not papers. IV. All takers are papers.

choices.a: Either I or II and IV follows
choices.c: Either I or II and III follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Many bucks are ducks. All trucks are ducks. Conclusions: I. Some ducks are not bucks. II. Some bucks are trucks. III. Some ducks are trucks. IV. All ducks are trucks.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All students are patriots. All Indians are patriots. Conclusions: I. Some Indians are Indians. II. Some Indians are students. III. All Indians are students. IV. All students are Indians.

choices.d: I and II and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some tables are chairs. All tables are lawns. Conclusions: I. Some chairs are lawns. II. Some chairs are not lawns. III. Some tables are lawns. IV. Some tables are not lawns.
